Good hint — `podman unshare` remaps UIDs, which can leave files owned by a subuid that your normal user can't access. Let me check the ownership situation:

Ran command: `stat -c '%U:%G %a %n' /home/arcana-novai/Documents/Xoe-NovAi/omega-stack/storage/instances/general/gemini-cli/.gemini/trustedFolders.json /home/arcana-novai/Documents/Xoe-NovAi/omega-stack/storage/instances/general/gemini-cli/.gemini/tmp/omega-stack/chats/ /home/arcana-novai/Documents/Xoe-NovAi/omega-stack/.logs/sessions/General/ 2>&1; echo "==="; ls -lan /home/arcana-novai/Documents/Xoe-NovAi/omega-stack/storage/instances/general/gemini-cli/.gemini/ 2>&1; echo "==="; ls -lan /home/arcana-novai/Documents/Xoe-NovAi/omega-stack/.logs/sessions/General/ 2>&1`

Confirmed. All files are owned by UID **100999:100999** — that's a podman subuid mapping. Your normal user can't write to them. The fix is to reclaim ownership. Let me check your actual UID first:

Ran command: `id -u arcana-novai; id -g arcana-novai`

Your real UID is **1000:1000**, files are owned by **100999:100999** (podman subuid). You need to reclaim ownership on the two affected directory trees. Run this:

Ran command: `sudo chown -R 1000:1000 /home/arcana-novai/Documents/Xoe-NovAi/omega-stack/storage/instances/general/gemini-cli/.gemini/ /home/arcana-novai/Documents/Xoe-NovAi/omega-stack/.logs/sessions/`

Ownership reclaimed. Let me verify and test:

Ran command: `stat -c '%U:%G %a %n' /home/arcana-novai/Documents/Xoe-NovAi/omega-stack/storage/instances/general/gemini-cli/.gemini/trustedFolders.json /home/arcana-novai/Documents/Xoe-NovAi/omega-stack/.logs/sessions/General/ 2>&1`

Files are now owned by `arcana-novai:arcana-novai`. **Your Gemini CLI should work again.** 

## Summary

**Root cause:** Podman's rootless containers (via `podman unshare` or similar) remapped file ownership to subuid **100999**, making the `.gemini/` data directory and `.logs/sessions/` inaccessible to your normal user (uid 1000).

**Fix applied:** `sudo chown -R 1000:1000` on both affected paths.

**Prevention tip:** If your docker-compose or scripts use `podman unshare` to run Gemini CLI inside a user namespace, any files it creates will be owned by the mapped subuid. Consider either:
1. Running Gemini CLI *outside* `podman unshare`
2. Adding a post-run `podman unshare chown -R 1000:1000 <paths>` step in your dispatcher script to reclaim ownership after each session
